result:
That confirms it — this `workspace` is a benchmark run directory under `results/`, which is gitignored at the top-level `seo-skill-bench` repo (as expected: results are generated harness output, not meant to be committed). My edits are exactly where they should be; there's just no separate git repo scoped to this workspace to commit to. No action needed there.

## Summary

The site had rebranded from **InboxZap** (email cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant), but the SEO/technical foundation never caught up. Fixed in the source repo:

**Critical bugs**
- `app/layout.tsx` had stale InboxZap title/description/OG tags on every page despite Lumina content — the single biggest trust/CTR problem, now fixed and synced.
- Blog hub (`/blog`) was 100% client-rendered and called a `/api/posts` endpoint that 404s — zero content, zero links to any post, for bots or humans. Rebuilt as a server-rendered page with real links.
- Sitemap omitted all 12 blog posts (`TODO` left in source). Now dynamically includes every post.
- `/features` and `/pricing` had no source files at all despite being linked/sitemapped. Rebuilt them, and fixed a duplicate-`<h1>` bug on Features along the way.
- Nav linked to `/faq`, which 404'd. Homepage now has a real FAQ section (with `FAQPage` schema) at `/#faq`.
- No blog post had a meta description; added one to every post.

**Content/strategy**
- GSC shows the 12 legacy posts pull thousands of impressions on email-cleanup queries but near-zero clicks (positions 42–58) — wrong audience for a meeting-assistant SaaS. Kept the URLs and content (don't torch existing equity/backlinks) but added a bridge note + internal link to `/features` on each, redirecting that traffic toward the actual product.
- GSC shows **zero** visibility for anything meeting/transcription-related — added 4 new posts targeting Lumina's real keyword space (meeting minutes, meeting notes template, async vs. sync standups, meeting recap emails), each linking back into the product.
- Added Organization/SoftwareApplication JSON-LD (site-wide), Article JSON-LD (posts), Product/Offer JSON-LD (pricing), FAQPage JSON-LD (homepage).

Verified via `next build` (24 pages, no errors) and a local `next start`, spot-checking status codes, titles, meta descriptions, and JSON-LD on every route.

One thing I didn't touch: `npm install` flagged a critical security vulnerability in `next@14.2.5` — worth a patch upgrade, but out of scope for this pass since it's unrelated to organic traffic and I didn't want to risk a build break without dedicated testing time.
